compiled_state.title: [pre-bash dispatcher]: ticket-create with triggering substrings in description -> blocked as command-execution
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

PreToolUse dispatcher guards (bypass-sentinel, record-test-status guard, tickets-tracker guard, etc.) perform substring match on the full Bash tool command string. When a legitimate ticket-create invocation includes a description that discusses the very patterns those guards exist to prohibit, the guards fire on the ticket-create call itself and block it — even though the ticket-create is not attempting the prohibited operation.

Expected: dispatcher guards fire only for actual command-execution attempts of the prohibited operation, not for descriptive text inside ticket payloads passed via the description flag.

Actual: the guards match any substring anywhere in the command string, including inside the quoted description argument. Filing a bug about the guarded pattern is blocked by the guard under investigation — a chicken-and-egg problem.

Reproduction (captured this session multiple times while filing adjacent tickets):
1. Description containing the phrase for the git short-form no-verify flag -> bypass-sentinel fires and blocks ticket creation.
2. Description containing a deletion phrase for TGS (the test-gate status artifact) -> bypass-sentinel fires on the deletion pattern and blocks.
3. Description containing the name of the writer script (record-TS hyphen status dot sh) -> record-TS guard fires and blocks.

Impact: priority 3 (DX/workflow friction). Agents filing bugs about guarded operations must obscure the triggering substrings (splitting with spaces, paraphrasing, removing examples, using placeholders), which reduces bug-report fidelity. Any description that accurately documents the prohibited pattern is unfile-able.

Root cause (hypothesis): the guards parse the tool input command as a single string and substring-match for trigger patterns. They do not distinguish between (a) the executable invocation portion of the command and (b) quoted arguments that are data payloads. Shell-aware parsing could extract just the program plus its arguments being executed, not the contents of quoted data strings.

Affected:
- plugins/dso/hooks/lib/pre-bash-functions.sh (multiple hook guard functions doing substring matches)
- plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/pre-bash.sh (the dispatcher itself)

Proposed fix direction (for investigation): either (a) the guards should ignore single-quoted and double-quoted argument payloads when substring-matching, or (b) recognize data-carrying invocations such as ticket create commands and apply a narrower guard strategy, or (c) only match the pattern on the executable path segment before any heredoc or quoted argument boundary.

Related: 1c89-68ee (cosmetic trailer errors on block path), 4344-7243 (sentinel allowlist for legitimate writer calls), 4945-35ed (safeguard governance gap).

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): Both pre-bash guards scan full command string. hook_tickets_tracker_bash_guard has ticket-CLI allowlist; hook_review_bypass_sentinel does NOT. Ticket CLI description args containing trigger substrings get false-positive blocked. Fix: add ticket-CLI exemption to sentinel with explicit guard against compound shell operators. ESCALATE: modifies safeguard file per CLAUDE.md rule 22 — requires user approval. Meta note: this very ticket comment was blocked on first attempt because my description contained a triggering substring, validating the bug.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: USER_APPROVAL: User approved safeguard file edits for this ticket (2026-04-19). Full fix-bug TDD process required.
comments.author: Test
